perturbation.original_text: The "Confidential Information" is that confidential, proprietary, and trade secret information being disclosed by the disclosing party described as (please be specific): (a) Yahoo Confidential Information (owned by Yahoo and any of its affiliates): Resume and Employment Related. (b) Participant Confidential Information: Resume and Employment Related.
perturbation.changed_text: The "Confidential Information" is that confidential, proprietary, and trade secret information being disclosed by the disclosing party described as (please be specific): (a) Yahoo Confidential Information (owned by Yahoo and any of its affiliates): Resume and Employment Related. This shall be the only information classified as Confidential Information under this agreement. (b) Participant Confidential Information: Resume and Employment Related.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "This shall be the only information classified as Confidential Information under this agreement" after the Yahoo Confidential Information definition, we create a contradiction. Section 1 originally defines 'Confidential Information' as including both Yahoo! Inc. and Participant information. This addition now implies that only Yahoo's information is covered, potentially excluding Restrac, Inc.'s information from the agreement's protections. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The "Confidential Information" is that confidential, proprietary, and trade secret information being disclosed by the disclosing party described as (please be specific): (a) Yahoo Confidential Information (owned by Yahoo and any of its affiliates): Resume and Employment Related. This shall be the only information classified as Confidential Information under this agreement. (b) Participant Confidential Information: Resume and Employment Related.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds the sentence "This shall be the only information classified as Confidential Information under this agreement" after the Yahoo Confidential Information definition. This contradicts the original definition of 'Confidential Information' which includes both Yahoo! Inc. and Participant information, by implying that only Yahoo's information is covered, potentially excluding the other party's information. The Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, showing the explicit contradiction being introduced.

perturbation.original_text: Except as set forth in this Section 2, all Confidential Information shall be in tangible form and shall be marked as Confidential or proprietary information of the disclosing party. If the Confidential Information is disclosed orally or visually, it shall be identified as such at the time of disclosure and confirmed in a writing to the recipient within thirty (30) days of such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as set forth in this Section 2, only Yahoo Confidential Information shall be in tangible form and shall be marked as Confidential or proprietary information of the disclosing party. If the Confidential Information is disclosed orally or visually, it shall be identified as such at the time of disclosure and confirmed in a writing to the recipient within thirty (30) days of such disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: By specifying that "only Yahoo Confidential Information shall be in tangible form and marked as Confidential", we contradict the agreement's initial intention to protect both Yahoo! Inc. and Restrac, Inc.'s confidential information. This change implies that Restrac's confidential information doesn't need to be marked or in tangible form, potentially stripping away key protective measures outlined in the clause, which creates an in-text contradiction within the section as it is the same section that it is contradicting.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as set forth in this Section 2, only Yahoo Confidential Information shall be in tangible form and shall be marked as Confidential or proprietary information of the disclosing party. If the Confidential Information is disclosed orally or visually, it shall be identified as such at the time of disclosure and confirmed in a writing to the recipient within thirty (30) days of such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text restricts the tangible form and marking requirements to only Yahoo Confidential Information, while the original text applied these requirements to all Confidential Information. This creates a contradiction because it implies Restrac's confidential information does not need to be marked or in tangible form, conflicting with the agreement's initial intention to protect both parties' confidential information as described in the explanation and causing an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each of the parties agrees that it will not make use of, disseminate, or in any way disclose any Confidential Information of the other party to any person, firm or business, except to the extent necessary for negotiations, discussions and consultations with personnel or authorized representatives of the other party and any purpose the other party may hereafter authorize in writing. Each of the parties agrees that it shall disclose Confidential Information of the other party only to those of its employees who need to know such information and who have previously agreed, either as a condition to employment or in order to obtain the Confidential Information, to be bound by terms and conditions substantially similar to those of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the parties agrees that it will not make use of, disseminate, or in any way disclose any Yahoo Confidential Information to any person, firm or business, except to the extent necessary for negotiations, discussions and consultations with personnel or authorized representatives of the other party and any purpose the other party may hereafter authorize in writing. Each of the parties agrees that it shall disclose Confidential Information of the other party only to those of its employees who need to know such information and who have previously agreed, either as a condition to employment or in order to obtain the Confidential Information, to be bound by terms and conditions substantially similar to those of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By specifying that the restriction on use and disclosure applies only to "Yahoo Confidential Information," the modified clause weakens the protection for Restrac's confidential information. While the second sentence mentions 'Confidential Information of the other party', its impact is diminished because the main operative clause restricts only Yahoo's information from being used, disseminated, or disclosed. This change undermines the mutuality of the agreement, contradicting the implied equal protection for both parties' confidential data as expressed in the whole contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of the parties agrees that it will not make use of, disseminate, or in any way disclose any Yahoo Confidential Information to any person, firm or business, except to the extent necessary for negotiations, discussions and consultations with personnel or authorized representatives of the other party and any purpose the other party may hereafter authorize in writing. Each of the parties agrees that it shall disclose Confidential Information of the other party only to those of its employees who need to know such information and who have previously agreed, either as a condition to employment or in order to obtain the Confidential Information, to be bound by terms and conditions substantially similar to those of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text ensures both parties' Confidential Information is protected. The changed text weakens this mutuality by specifying that only 'Yahoo Confidential Information' is restricted from use and disclosure in the first sentence. While the second sentence refers to 'Confidential Information of the other party', the initial restriction focusing solely on Yahoo's information creates a contradiction in the agreement. This contradicts the original intent of the clause which was to provide equal protection for both parties' confidential data, as outlined in the contract as a whole. Thus, there is an in-text contradiction as the modified clause's primary restriction targets only Yahoo's information, undermining the implied equal protection for both parties' data, as expressed in the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: There shall be no liability for disclosure or use of Confidential Information which is (a) in the public domain through no fault of the receiving party (b) rightfully received from a third party without any obligation of confidentiality, (c) rightfully known to the receiving party without any limitation on use or disclosure prior to its receipt from the disclosing party, (d) independently developed by the receiving party, (e) generally made available to third parties without any restriction on disclosure, or (f) communicated in response to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as otherwise required by law, or as necessary to establish the rights of either party under this Agreement (provided that the party so disclosing has provided the other party with a reasonable opportunity to seek protective legal treatment for such Confidential Information).
perturbation.changed_text: There shall be no liability for disclosure or use of Yahoo Confidential Information which is (a) in the public domain through no fault of the receiving party (b) rightfully received from a third party without any obligation of confidentiality, (c) rightfully known to the receiving party without any limitation on use or disclosure prior to its receipt from the disclosing party, (d) independently developed by the receiving party, (e) generally made available to third parties without any restriction on disclosure, or (f) communicated in response to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as otherwise required by law, or as necessary to establish the rights of either party under this Agreement (provided that the party so disclosing has provided the other party with a reasonable opportunity to seek protective legal treatment for such Confidential Information).
perturbation.explanation: By limiting the exceptions to liability to disclosures of "Yahoo Confidential Information", we create a loophole where Restrac's confidential information, if disclosed under the circumstances in (a)-(f), might still lead to liability. This is because the section specifies 'Yahoo Confidential Information' and, as defined earlier with other changes, has created separation. This undermines the intent of the NDA to protect both parties, causing in-text contradiction in the purpose of the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: There shall be no liability for disclosure or use of Yahoo Confidential Information which is (a) in the public domain through no fault of the receiving party (b) rightfully received from a third party without any obligation of confidentiality, (c) rightfully known to the receiving party without any limitation on use or disclosure prior to its receipt from the disclosing party, (d) independently developed by the receiving party, (e) generally made available to third parties without any restriction on disclosure, or (f) communicated in response to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as otherwise required by law, or as necessary to establish the rights of either party under this Agreement (provided that the party so disclosing has provided the other party with a reasonable opportunity to seek protective legal treatment for such Confidential Information).
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'There shall be no liability for disclosure or use of Confidential Information'. The changed text narrows this to 'There shall be no liability for disclosure or use of Yahoo Confidential Information'. This creates a contradiction because it implies that there *is* liability for disclosure of Restrac's confidential information under circumstances (a)-(f), conflicting with the implied intent of the agreement, and with the contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text, showing that there is no allowance for Restrac's confidental information under the listed circumstances.

perturbation.original_text: "Residual Information" shall mean any Confidential Information of the disclosing party which may be retained in intangible form in the minds of those individuals of the receiving party who have had proper access to such Confidential Information. Notwithstanding anything else in this Agreement, the receiving party shall be free to use any Residual Information for any purpose whatsoever, including, without limitation, the development of its own products, provided that such party shall not be entitled to disclose Residual Information to any third parties unless such disclosure is in the course of, or as part of, any disclosure of its own products or their development.
perturbation.changed_text: "Residual Information" shall mean any Yahoo Confidential Information of the disclosing party which may be retained in intangible form in the minds of those individuals of the receiving party who have had proper access to such Confidential Information. Notwithstanding anything else in this Agreement, the receiving party shall be free to use any Residual Information for any purpose whatsoever, including, without limitation, the development of its own products, provided that such party shall not be entitled to disclose Residual Information to any third parties unless such disclosure is in the course of, or as part of, any disclosure of its own products or their development.
perturbation.explanation: By narrowing the definition of "Residual Information" to only "Yahoo Confidential Information", this clause suggests that Restrac's confidential information, even if retained in intangible form, might not be subject to the same provisions allowing free use by the receiving party. While ostensibly allowing free use of residual information, the restriction to "Yahoo Confidential Information" creates uncertainty about how Restrac's residual information should be handled, contradicting the principle of equal treatment intended in the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Residual Information" shall mean any Yahoo Confidential Information of the disclosing party which may be retained in intangible form in the minds of those individuals of the receiving party who have had proper access to such Confidential Information. Notwithstanding anything else in this Agreement, the receiving party shall be free to use any Residual Information for any purpose whatsoever, including, without limitation, the development of its own products, provided that such party shall not be entitled to disclose Residual Information to any third parties unless such disclosure is in the course of, or as part of, any disclosure of its own products or their development.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Residual Information' as 'any Confidential Information of the disclosing party', while the changed text narrows it to 'Yahoo Confidential Information'. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, re-stating this narrowed definition. This creates a contradiction because the changed text implies that Restrac's confidential information, even if retained intangibly, might not be subject to the same free-use provisions. This contradicts the original intent of potentially having equal treatment in the NDA, making enforcement unclear, and hence, a strong in-text contradiction exists, because it introduces ambiguity where the contradicted clause expects certainty regarding the definition of Residual Information.

perturbation.original_text: Each of the parties agrees that it shall treat all Confidential Information of the other party with the same degree of care as it accords to its own Confidential Information and each of the parties represents that it exercises reasonable care to protect its own Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the parties agrees that it shall treat all Yahoo Confidential Information of the other party with the same degree of care as it accords to its own Confidential Information and each of the parties represents that it exercises reasonable care to protect its own Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "Confidential Information of the other party" to "Yahoo Confidential Information of the other party", the contract now specifies the degree of care obligation is only directed to protect Yahoo's Confidential information, creating a contradiction with the intended protection of the NDA and leaving an uncertainty of the level of protection that Restrac's Confidential information will have. The implied mutual protection of confidential data is now diminished, which is contrary to the nature of a mutual NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of the parties agrees that it shall treat all Yahoo Confidential Information of the other party with the same degree of care as it accords to its own Confidential Information and each of the parties represents that it exercises reasonable care to protect its own Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters 'Confidential Information of the other party' to 'Yahoo Confidential Information of the other party'. This creates a contradiction because the degree of care obligation is now only directed to protect Yahoo's confidential information, while the contradicted text implies mutual protection. This narrows the scope of protection and creates a strong inconsistency, because Restrac's confidential information may not be protected to the same degree as Yahoo's confidential information.
